alignments.candidate_evidence: No demonstrated ML experimentation, model training, or empirical research background. Candidate's tooling skills (SQL, BigQuery, Looker) support data analysis but not experimental design or model evaluation.
alignments.job_requirement: Build and run elegant and thorough machine learning experiments

alignments.candidate_evidence: Python at basic level, actively learning (data-analysis / pandas track). No evidence of production Python scripts, experimental codebases, or ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow).
alignments.job_requirement: Conduct all interviews in Python; write scripts and run experiments

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years of Google strategy and operations with transformation program authorship and executive communication. No peer-reviewed research, academic publication, or AI safety research writing.
alignments.job_requirement: Contribute ideas, figures, and writing to research papers, blog posts, and talks

alignments.candidate_evidence: No software engineering background. Designs and directs LLM coding agents (agentic tooling) and manages complex transformation and operational systems, but not hands-on research engineering or model development.
alignments.job_requirement: Have significant software, ML, or research engineering experience

alignments.candidate_evidence: Directed LLM agents for production systems and owns evaluation methodology and benchmarking. This is AI-systems tooling direction, not empirical research project contribution.
alignments.job_requirement: Some experience contributing to empirical AI research projects

alignments.candidate_evidence: Has worked with LLMs through agentic-tooling direction. No demonstrated RL, Kubernetes, or complex shared-codebase experience.
alignments.job_requirement: Strong candidates may have experience with LLMs, reinforcement learning, and Kubernetes clusters

gaps.gap: No production ML engineering, empirical research, or model-training background. Role requires hands-on ML experimentation; candidate has strategic tooling direction only.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate could shadow or intern on a single empirical project, but would require significant ramp on ML frameworks, experimental design, and safety-research methodology.

gaps.gap: Python at basic/learning level; JD conducts interviews in Python and requires scripting for experiments and evaluation. Candidate learns pandas-track data analysis, not ML engineering.
gaps.mitigation: Intensive upskilling in PyTorch/TensorFlow, data loading, and experiment harnesses would take months. Fast-moving collaborative projects would struggle with proficiency gaps.

gaps.gap: No research publication, peer review, or AI safety research authorship. Role emphasizes papers, blog posts, and alignment research contribution.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's transformation and executive communication skills could transfer to research communication, but no safety research domain knowledge.

gaps.gap: No RL, Kubernetes, complex shared-codebase, or frontier-model evaluation experience. These are strong-candidate signals in the JD.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate could develop familiarity if offered mentorship, but would remain in catch-up mode on specialized tooling.

gaps.gap: Limited AI safety research familiarity. JD assumes knowledge of alignment failures, misalignment stress-testing, and safety-technique robustness testing.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would need to read foundational safety research and undertake significant domain learning before contributing independently.

hard_blockers.evidence: "Have significant software, ML, or research engineering experience" and "conduct all interviews in Python." Candidate has no software engineering, no ML engineering, and Python at basic/learning level only. JD explicitly requires interviews be conducted in Python, signaling that production-level Python fluency is a gating skill. Candidate's honest gap profile confirms "No production software-engineering background" and "Python basic and actively learning."

output.level_rationale: JD requires "significant software, ML, or research engineering experience" and authoring research papers as a strong. Salary band (£260–370k) and emphasis on collaborative empirical research suggest senior IC (L5) rather than entry (L3–L4). However, no explicit years minimum stated, and company stage/size context unclear, limiting confidence.
output.summary: Research Engineer role at Anthropic requires hands-on ML/AI safety research, empirical experimentation (Python interviews, multi-agent RL, LLM evaluation), and research-paper authorship. Candidate brings eight years of strategy and operations at Google—advanced tooling (SQL, BigQuery, Looker), process design, stakeholder management, and agentic-LLM direction—but lacks production ML engineering, research authorship, and empirical AI safety background. Role is outside Strategy and Operations / Product Ops family; deprioritized as deep technical research engineering without strategic or transformation scope.
uncertainties: JD does not state explicit minimum years of experience; interpreted 'significant' as L5 senior IC, but could be L4 in context of rapid early-stage growth.
Candidate's agentic-LLM direction and evaluation methodology ownership is not production ML but could be undersold; unclear whether JD would view this as partial credit for 'ML experience.'
London location requires 25% on-site; visa sponsorship is available but uncertain whether candidate's visa status is already resolved.
JD encourages applications even without every qualification; however, Python-interview gating and empirical research core mission create structural misalignment, not just preference gap.
